Variables.Name: MaxZeroingThreshold
Variables.Description: Maximum threshold to use when replacing a component with zero. Lower values retain more keys, but yield less compression.

Variables.Name: MaxPosDiffBitwise
Variables.Description: Maximum position difference to use when testing if an animation key may be removed. Lower values retain more keys, but yield less compression.

Variables.Name: MaxAngleDiffBitwise
Variables.Description: Maximum angle difference to use when testing if an animation key may be removed. Lower values retain more keys, but yield less compression.

Variables.Name: MaxScaleDiffBitwise
Variables.Description: Maximum position difference to use when testing if an animation key may be removed. Lower values retain more keys, but yield less compression.

Variables.Name: AllowedRotationFormats
Variables.Description: Which encoding formats is the per-track compressor allowed to try on rotation keys

Variables.Name: AllowedTranslationFormats
Variables.Description: Which encoding formats is the per-track compressor allowed to try on translation keys

Variables.Name: AllowedScaleFormats
Variables.Description: Which encoding formats is the per-track compressor allowed to try on scale keys

Variables.Name: bResampleAnimation
Variables.Description: If true, resample the animation to ResampleFramerate frames per second

Variables.Name: ResampledFramerate
Variables.Description: When bResampleAnimation is true, this defines the desired framerate

Variables.Name: MinKeysForResampling
Variables.Description: Animations with fewer keys than MinKeysForResampling will not be resampled.

Variables.Name: bUseAdaptiveError
Variables.Description: If true, adjust the error thresholds based on the 'height' within the skeleton

Variables.Name: bUseOverrideForEndEffectors
Variables.Description: If true, uses MinEffectorDiff as the threhsold for end effectors

Variables.Name: TrackHeightBias
Variables.Description: A bias added to the track height before using it to calculate the adaptive error

Variables.Name: ParentingDivisor
Variables.Description:
Reduces the error tolerance the further up the tree that a key occurs
	  EffectiveErrorTolerance = Max(BaseErrorTolerance  Power(ParentingDivisor, Max(Height+Bias,0)  ParentingDivisorExponent), ZeroingThreshold)
	  Only has an effect bUseAdaptiveError is true

Variables.Name: ParentingDivisorExponent
Variables.Description:
Reduces the error tolerance the further up the tree that a key occurs
	  EffectiveErrorTolerance = Max(BaseErrorTolerance  Power(ParentingDivisor, Max(Height+Bias,0)  ParentingDivisorExponent), ZeroingThreshold)
	  Only has an effect bUseAdaptiveError is true

Variables.Name: bUseAdaptiveError2
Variables.Description:
If true, the adaptive error system will determine how much error to allow for each track, based on the
	  error introduced in end effectors due to errors in the track.

Variables.Name: RotationErrorSourceRatio
Variables.Description:
This ratio determines how much error in end effector rotation can come from a given track's rotation error or translation error.
	  If 1, all of it must come from rotation error, if 0.5, half can come from each, and if 0.0, all must come from translation error.

Variables.Name: TranslationErrorSourceRatio
Variables.Description:
This ratio determines how much error in end effector translation can come from a given track's rotation error or translation error.
	  If 1, all of it must come from rotation error, if 0.5, half can come from each, and if 0.0, all must come from translation error.

Variables.Name: ScaleErrorSourceRatio
Variables.Description:
This ratio determines how much error in end effector scale can come from a given track's rotation error or scale error.
	  If 1, all of it must come from rotation error, if 0.5, half can come from each, and if 0.0, all must come from scale error.

Variables.Name: MaxErrorPerTrackRatio
Variables.Description: A fraction that determines how much of the total error budget can be introduced by any particular track

Variables.Name: PerturbationProbeSize
Variables.Description: How big of a perturbation should be made when probing error propagation
